title: Dynamic code execution should not be vulnerable to injection attacks
description: Code injections occur when applications allow the dynamic execution of code instructions from untrusted data. An attacker can influence the behavior of the targeted application and modify it to get access to sensitive data. What is the potential impact? An attacker exploiting a dynamic code injection vulnerability will be able to execute arbitrary code in the context of the vulnerable application.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detect Assembly.Load, Assembly.LoadFrom, Assembly.LoadFile, or CompileAssemblyFromSource with user input
logic.triggering_conditions: Call to Assembly.Load/LoadFrom/LoadFile or CompileAssemblyFromSource with non-constant input
logic.exclusion_conditions: Exclude calls with string literal constants
logic.reasoning: Dynamic assembly loading and code compilation with user input can lead to arbitrary code execution. Attackers can provide malicious assemblies or code to execute arbitrary commands.
